title: Resistance
short: How strongly a component opposes current, R = U / I, measured in ohms (Ω).
full:
Resistance measures how much a component opposes the flow of current: for a given voltage, a higher resistance means a smaller current, R = U/I. The lost electrical energy turns into heat, which is how kettles and incandescent bulbs work.

The resistance of a wire depends on the material and its shape — longer and thinner means more resistance, R = ρ·L/S.

The SI unit is the ohm (Ω); 1 Ω = 1 V/A.
